passage.title: GT Set #9001
passage.text: Our class has a weather board by the door. Every morning, one student looks outside before the bell. The student moves the sun, cloud, or rain card to match the sky. Our teacher asks us to say the weather in English and write the date. Sometimes we compare today's card with yesterday's card and talk about small changes. We like checking the board because it changes a little every day.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the class uses the weather board each day.
The first sentence says the weather board is by the door.

questions.prompt: When does one student look outside?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says the student looks outside before the bell.

questions.prompt: What does the student move on the board?
questions.rationale: The passage says the student moves the sun, cloud, or rain card.

questions.prompt: What does match mean in the third sentence?
questions.rationale: Match means the card should show the same kind of weather as the sky.

questions.prompt: What does changes mean in the last sentence?
questions.rationale: Changes means the board becomes different as the weather changes.
